plainSummary: A practice matters when it helps us come back changed, humble, and useful in ordinary life.
atAGlance: The real test comes after the bright moment. A practice has done its work when we can return without boasting or clinging. Some disciplines must be kept, some released, and some made into daily conduct. Watch whether the person becomes steadier, kinder, and less hungry to prove themselves.
keyPoints: Meaning is tested by how we speak, work, and treat others afterward.
The danger is making a practice into proof of personal worth.
Test whether ordinary conduct becomes steadier when the experience has passed.
